acceptanceSignals: Rules trigger alerts for expiry risk, on-time degradation, satisfaction drop, and complaint spikes.
Each alert has owner, status lifecycle, and closure note.
Escalation and SLA status are queryable and auditable.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Anomaly Alert Workflow in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The Anomaly Alert Workflow in this workspace satisfies the active policy: it delivers rule-based anomaly detection (expiry risk, on-time degradation, satisfaction drop, complaint spike), tracked owner/lifecycle/closure evidence, and queryable SLA/escalation with audit traceability; formal HTTP behavior is synchronized with OpenAPI and generated TypeScript client artifacts, with contract conformance checks (including route-to-spec parity and drift gating) present.

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The anomaly alert workflow satisfies the active policy: rule-based alerts cover the four required anomaly signals, remediation lifecycle enforces owner/status/closure evidence, escalation and SLA are queryable, and associated high-risk writes emit immutable audit evidence with actor identity, role, entity, reason, timestamp, and correlationId for investigation chaining.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The current anomaly alert implementation satisfies the active policy for this feature: it provides rule-based detection for expiry risk, on-time degradation, satisfaction drop, and complaint spikes; enforces owner assignment and lifecycle transitions with mandatory closure note/evidence for close; exposes escalation and SLA state as query filters/fields; and records anomaly lifecycle actions in auditable trail entities/actions without retaining anomaly-specific legacy/fallback routes or DTO branches.

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: The workspace satisfies this feature review: anomaly rules cover expiry risk/on-time degradation/satisfaction drop/complaint spike, alerts enforce owner/lifecycle/closure evidence, escalation and SLA state are queryable and backed by immutable audit events, and the active payroll/order/audit retention policy is technically enforced via purge workflows (including scheduled runtime jobs), encryption and role-access test coverage, and audit-investigation traceability for purge outcomes.

excludedApproaches: Alert-only notifications with no owner/workflow tracking.
Closing alerts without accountable disposition records.
Hardcoding non-configurable thresholds with no governance metadata.
executionConstraints: Provide rule-based anomaly detection and tracked remediation workflow.
Support owner assignment, SLA timing, and closure evidence in alert lifecycle.
Finalize ticket-integration depth per remaining clarification without blocking baseline workflow.
name: Anomaly Alert Workflow
rationale: Committee governance requires actionable anomaly handling, not passive reporting.
summary: Deliver anomaly detection with ownership and remediation tracking.
